raw_results.query: best practices autonomous LLM agent memory management
new methods for LLM agent orchestration and multi-agent coordination

suggestions: Self-Reflection and In-Loop Feedback**: Agents critique their own outputs at inference time using methods like Reflexion (iterative refinement), Self-Refine, Self-Consistency (majority voting over reasoning chains), and Self-Rewarding Language Models (self-scoring outputs for RL rewards).[1][4] This is the simplest, most effective starting point for passive reliability gains.[1][4]

Self-Generated Data and Curricula**: Agents create their own training examples, problems, and demonstrations, then fine-tune on verified traces (e.g., STaR, SELF, STaSC) or self-challenging in-context examples. Imitation learning evolves through agent-generated demos shared in peer groups.[1][3]

Self-Adapting Models**: Agents fine-tune parameters, edit weights, or refine internal representations like memory (merge/forget data) and prompts based on outcomes. Techniques include reflective tree search for simulating and pruning paths.[1][3][5]
